Business_Interpretation: Total spend is the strongest churn predictor. Low-spend customers are the primary risk group.
Repeat buyers are very unlikely to churn. High-frequency customers get extreme protection; low-frequency adds only mild risk.
Customers placing larger orders show more commitment and lower churn probability.
Broader product engagement signals stickiness. Cross-category buyers are harder to lose.

Direction: More days inactive → higher churn risk
Business_Interpretation: Expected direction but only 5th in importance. Signal partially absorbed by Monetary and Frequency.
Spending consistency does not differentiate churners, likely due to sparse order histories (median 3–5 orders).
Normalised disengagement signal adds no value beyond raw Recency.
Purchase timing regularity carries no signal after Frequency and Recency are accounted for.
